title: nymphoides parvifolia
intro: Nymphoides parvifolia, also known as small leaf water lily or small water lily, is a type of aquatic plant native to Australia and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Nymphoides parvifolia include reducing inflammation, relieving fever, and treating various skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Nymphoides parvifolia include infusion, decoction, and tincture, which can be used to make tea, capsules, or topical creams for various health conditions.
